normative_execution.ordering: ascending (tick,sequence); sequence is unique and contiguous from zero
normative_execution.fixed_tick_rule: apply every event whose tick equals the current authoritative tick before advancing systems
normative_execution.case_isolation: each case begins from its matching case_state; no state is shared between cases
normative_execution.integer_rule: all *_milli values use signed integer arithmetic with checked overflow
normative_execution.autonomous_tick_rule: world, settlement, faction, crisis, and expedition systems advance once per declared fixed tick even when events is empty
normative_execution.target_resolution_rule: resolve every scripted command payload.target by exact logical_target_id before tick zero; canonical bindings select resolved_id in the base run and the matching case_resolutions entry in a case run; scenario selectors resolve only by their declared selector_kind and selector_value
normative_execution.unresolved_target_rule: reject the scenario before tick zero on a missing or duplicate binding, an unreferenced binding, an unknown selector, a missing case resolution, a kind mismatch, or an ID absent from the hash-bound starting-state or content-set artifact; never infer, fabricate, or fall back
normative_execution.target_binding_hash_rule: target_bindings_sha256 is SHA-256 of canonical JSON for the complete ordered target_bindings array

bindings.counts-semantics: runner-envelope; authoritative counts live per case
